title: Skip-Gram Architecture
concept: Skip-gram takes a single word and predicts the surrounding context words, performing better on rare words and large datasets.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of the Skip-gram architecture?

options.text: Predicting surrounding context words from a single word
Skipping random words to shorten a sentence
Translating words from one language to another

exercises.explain: Skip-gram uses a single word to predict the words that surround it in a sentence.

cards.title: Predicting the Crowd
cards.body: Have you ever heard a single word and immediately pictured the scene around it? If someone says "beach", you might automatically think of "sand", "ocean", and "sun". Skip-gram teaches computers to do the exact same thing!

cards.title: What is Skip-Gram?
cards.body: The Skip-gram architecture is a way of training computers to understand words. Instead of using a sentence to guess a missing word, Skip-gram does the opposite: it takes a single word and tries to predict which words are likely to appear around it.

cards.title: The Puzzle Piece Analogy
cards.body: Imagine you are looking at a single puzzle piece in the middle of a table. Instead of finding where it fits, your job is to guess what the surrounding pieces look like. That's Skip-gram! It starts with one word (the middle piece) and guesses the neighbors.

cards.title: Skip-Gram in Action
cards.body: In the sentence "The cat sat on the mat", if our target word is "sat", Skip-gram will try to predict the surrounding words like "cat" and "on". Because it focuses on these pairs, it gets really good at learning about rare words that don't show up very often.

cards.title: Not Just for Common Words
cards.body: You might think Skip-gram is only for common words because they appear everywhere. Actually, it's the opposite! Skip-gram is famous for performing exceptionally well on rare and unusual words because it treats every single word-context pair as a valuable learning moment.

cards.title: Visualizing Skip-Gram
cards.body: Here is how Skip-gram compares to other models that predict words. Skip-gram focuses on expanding outward from a single word.

exercises.prompt: What is a major advantage of using the Skip-gram architecture?

options.text: It performs better on rare words and large datasets
It only works on short sentences
It completely ignores the order of words
It is much slower and less accurate than older models

exercises.explain: Skip-gram is highly valued because it performs well on large datasets and is excellent at capturing the meaning of rare words.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Skip-gram takes a group of surrounding words and uses them to predict a single target word in the middle.
exercises.explain: Skip-gram does the exact opposite: it takes a single word and predicts the context around it.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete how Skip-gram works:
exercises.sentence: Skip-gram starts with a single target word and predicts the surrounding [blank] words.
exercises.explain: Skip-gram takes one target word and attempts to predict its surrounding context words.

exercises.prompt: Two AI models are describing the input and output of the Skip-gram architecture. Which model is correct?

options.text: AI Model A says: 'It takes five context words and compresses them into a single middle word.'
AI Model B says: 'It takes a single target word and predicts multiple surrounding context words.'
AI Model C says: 'It skips the target word entirely and only looks at punctuation.'

exercises.explain: AI Model B is correct. Skip-gram takes a single target word as input and outputs predictions for the surrounding context words.

exercises.prompt: You are building a custom search engine and need to capture the meaning of rare industry terms. Why should you choose the Skip-gram architecture over other methods?

options.text: It averages all surrounding words to guess a single missing middle word.
It takes one word and predicts multiple surrounding context words, favoring rare words.
It skips every second word in a sentence to speed up training times.
It only works on small datasets with common, simple vocabulary.

exercises.explain: Skip-gram is great at predicting context words from a single target word, making it highly effective at learning accurate representations for rare words since it maximizes the training instances generated from each occurrence.
